calendar.matters:
PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
Off Calendar Feb-01-2010 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
Off Calendar Jan-04-2010 Continued To Feb-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for Valeraino Pereyra.

Mr. Dimitriou presented an amended declaration to the Court noting that a sentence in the declaration was incomplete and at the request of the Examiner he had corrected the error. In addition, he stated that there were no assets in the Estate, that he was never told by the litigation attorney that they had received a request for money by CCSF, that the Bureau of Delinquent Revenue had been served with today's hearing, and that he could get the proof of service on the Bureau of Delinquent Revenue to the Court by noon today. The Court returned the copy of the second amended report to Mr. Dimitriou and continued the matter to February 1, 2010. Mr. Dimitriou stated that he would give notice to the Bureau of Delinquent Revenue of the February 1, 2010 hearing.

Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
Off Calendar Dec-07-2009 Continued To Jan-04-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Domitriou for Valeraino Pereyra.

Mr. Dimitriou apologized to the Court stating that his focus was on a trial he had in Department 206, submitted a proposed order and stated that he preferred to re-notice the hearing so that he could provide notice to all entitled Parties rather than re-construct the proof of service. In addition, he stated that he would contact the Delinquency Tax Revenue Department with regard to the creditor's claim and that he had not located the family members in the Philippines. The matter was continued to January 4, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for re-notice and for filing of the affidavit of mailing. If Mr. Dimitriou does not file a declaration verifying the information he provided to the Court at the November 2, 2009 hearing by that date, contact the family members in the Philippines and the Buraeu of Delinquent Tax Revenue, sanctions in the amount of $25.00 will be imposed until the information is received.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
Off Calendar Nov-23-2009 Continued To Dec-07-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
Off Calendar Nov-02-2009 Continued To Nov-23-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for Valeraino Pererya. Andrew Dimitriou's Law Clerk was also present.

Mr. Dimitriou stated that he had received the Examiner's notes, that he thought he had submitted a proposed order, that he will re-draft and submit an order, and he will also submit the endorsed proof of service to the Court. In addition, Mr. Dimitriou stated that he did not notice the Bureau of Delinquent Revenue because he never received that creditor's claim, that all the bills from the accident pursuant to the spousal property claim have been paid, that he has contacted Brian Larsen, that he hasn't received a response from him regarding this creditor's claim, and again that he was unaware of this claim until he received the Examiner's notes. In regard to Mr. Pererya, Mr. Dimitriou stated that he hasn't been able to get in touch with him, that he thought he was returning from the Philippines, that he can't get his signature on the petition, that he sent a request for his signature in April, that he had spoken with Mr. Larsen in July to confirm Mr. Pererya's address, that he overnighted the document with paid return postage, and he still received nothing. The Court stated that it wanted Counsel to submit the following items: 1) a declaration regarding verification based upon his statements made today, 2) an update, under penalty of perjury, that the creditor's claim has been paid, 3) a/the proof of service, and 4) a proposed order. The matter was continued to November 23, 2009 for compliance.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy McConico.

PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for Valeraino Pereyra.

The matter was continued to July 15, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for proof of filing of the amended report and petition to terminate the estate.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Off Calendar May-05-2009 Off Calendar

Off Calendar May-05-2009 Continued To May-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-24-2009 Set For Order To Show Cause On May-05-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person.

An Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Attorney for failure to appear and for failure to file the petition to close the estate. The return date is May 5, 2009 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-10-2009 Continued To Mar-24-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for Valeraino Pereyra.

Mr. Dimitriou stated that he priority mailed the report to the Philippines, that his Client was not returning from the Philippines, and that his Client did not sign the report. The matter was continued to March 24, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for proof of filing of the Petition To Close the Estate.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-04-2009 Continued To Mar-10-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for the Petitioner.

Mr. Dimitriou stated that all of the estate debts had been paid and that he was unable to get a signature from his Client. The Court directed Mr. Dimitriou to send the report to the last known address in the Philippines for verification from his Client in the manner most likely to be received and in a timely manner. A faxed signature will be sufficient. The matter was continued to March 10, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on verification of the report. Mr. Dimitriou will file a declaration as to his efforts and the outcome by March 3, 2009.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: R. Noguera/J. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Garrido.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-17-2008 Continued To Feb-04-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Demetrious Dimitriou for Dimitriou and Associates.

Mr. Dimitriou requested 30 to 45 day continuance stating that his client was in the Philippines and that he needed to sign the verification. The matter was continued to February 4, 2009.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Aquino.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-03-2008 Continued To Dec-17-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Nov-13-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for the Petitioner.

Mr. Dimitriou informed the Court that the original proof of service had not been filed, that he found it in his file, and that he filed it this morning. In addition, he stated that the City and County Tax Collector and the Philippine Consulate had been given notice of the October 14, 2008 hearing date. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Leong.

Off Calendar May-12-2008 Continued To Jun-02-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Julie Chan for the Estate of Zenaida Liquigan.

Ms. Chan reported that she was not able to locate the spouse in the Philippines and that she may withdraw the request for filing of the Spousal Property Petition. The Court stated that something needed to be filed in regard to closing the estate, the filing of the inventory and appraisal or status. If nothing is filed by the next hearing date, the Court will consider suspending the powers. The matter was continued to June 2, 2008.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.
